extra.reasoning: The property "globally(opted_out implies not sent)" holds on all reachable executions. The only transition that sets sent:=true is [t2] (send_attempt), which is guarded by "not opted_out" and does not modify opted_out; thus immediately after [t2] the state has opted_out=false, sent=true, satisfying the implication vacuously. The only transition that sets opted_out:=true is [t1] (optout), and it simultaneously sets sent:=false, so the post-state has opted_out=true, sent=false — satisfying the property. No transition ever sets opted_out back to false, so once opted_out is true it remains true, and [t2] (the sole setter of sent=true, requiring not opted_out) can never fire again; meanwhile [t3] and [t4] both force sent:=false. Therefore the invariant (opted_out implies not sent) holds at the initial state (false implies anything) and is preserved by every transition: [t1] yields sent=false; [t2] keeps opted_out=false (vacuous); [t3] yields sent=false; [t4] yields sent=false. By induction it holds in every reachable state, including for executions up to 14 events. No counterexample exists.
